ocr.text: In the Asia-Pacific professional services landscape, the utilization rate serves as a crucial indicator of operational efficiency and
profitability across various practices.
UtilizationRatebyPractice  PracticeArea  Revenue(AUD)  Utilization(%)  NetIncome(AUD)  EmployeeCount  HoursWorked  CAPEX(AUD)  ProfitMargin(%)
68.5%  $7M  150  25,000  $3M  23.3%  TaxAdvisory  [HIDDEN]  [HIDDEN]  $4M
Marketing  $15M  72.0%  $3M  90  15,000  [HIDDEN]  20.0%  Total  $185M
As illustrated in the accompanying table, the Consulting practice reported an advisory fee income of AUD $45M, achieving a
utilization rate of 75.3%. This performance underscores the effectiveness of resource allocation within the practice. In contrast,
the Audit practice demonstrated a utilization rate of 65.0%, with an advisory fee income of AUD $30M, suggesting potential
areas for improvement in efficiency. Notably, the metrics related to efficiency ratios, such as the Asset Turnover, which stands at
12.0%, and the Payables Turnover at 5.0, further highlight the operational dynamics within these practices. Additionally, the
Marketing division, with an advisory fee income of AUD $1.5M, reflects a strategic focus on enhancing client engagement,
while achieving a notable metric of $20M in overall fee generation. The data also reveals that the efficiency ratio for the
Consulting practice reached an impressive $10M with 140,000 hours billed, indicating a strong correlation between service
delivery and income generation.
